papers_mined.title: Bògòlanfini (Wikipedia)
papers_mined.note: Malian Bambara mud cloth: cloth first dyed in n'gallama leaf bath, then mud applied to RESIST areas; mud-cloth chemistry exploits fermented mud reaction → brown color remains after mud washed off.

papers_mined.title: Bamana Mudcloth UWM Art Collection
papers_mined.note: Mud cloth is REVERSE/negative-space technique — dye removed where mud was applied; pattern emerges as untouched cloth.

papers_mined.title: Bogolanfini Craft Atlas
papers_mined.note: Mud chemistry: fermented for up to a year in clay jar; reacts with treated dye to fix pattern.

papers_mined.title: Bogolanfini Leila Atelier Textile Stories
papers_mined.note: Patterns rich in cultural significance — historical events, animals, proverbs.
